mission.title: Microcks: Fix "Error storing daily statistics" (IncorrectResultSizeDataAccessException)
mission.description: Resolves the DailyStatisticsFeeder error where MongoDB returns non-unique results for daily invocation statistics. This occurs when running multiple Microcks replicas that create duplicate statistics documents for the same day/service combination.

steps.title: Identify the error
steps.description:
Check Microcks logs for the statistics error:
```bash
kubectl logs -n microcks -l app=microcks --tail=200 | grep -i 'DailyStatisticsFeeder\|IncorrectResultSize\|daily statistics'
```
The error looks like:
```
org.springframework.dao.IncorrectResultSizeDataAccessException: Query returned non unique result
```
This occurs under load when multiple replicas write statistics for the same service/day/version key.

steps.title: Remove duplicate statistics documents
Create a unique index to prevent recurrence
Verify fix
steps.description:
Restart Microcks and generate some mock invocations to verify statistics are stored correctly:
```bash
kubectl rollout restart deployment/microcks -n microcks
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=microcks -n microcks --timeout=120s

# Check logs are clean
kubectl logs -n microcks -l app=microcks --tail=50 | grep -i 'DailyStatisticsFeeder\|IncorrectResultSize'
```
No errors should appear for daily statistics operations.

resolution.summary: The daily statistics error is caused by duplicate MongoDB documents created by concurrent Microcks replicas. Fixed by removing duplicates and adding a unique compound index on (day, serviceName, serviceVersion).
